steps.title: Attach the opening modifier
steps.content: The sentence begins with the modifier "Established in 1936 by African American novelist Richard Wright." The noun immediately after that modifier must be the thing Wright established: the South Side Writers Group.

steps.title: Keep both clauses clear
steps.content: B and D make writers the modified subject, and C makes Chicago the modified subject. The correct construction names the South Side Writers Group right after the modifier and then uses a semicolon before the independent clause beginning "it would become." The answer is A.
